artifacts.summary: Research findings for Task 52: Sleep Inhibition for Claude Code/Opencode. Analyzed existing disabled inhibitor service, identified Task 50 pgrep self-match as a blocker, confirmed systemd-inhibit as the correct mechanism for Nix/Wayland systems, and recommended consolidating Task 50 and 52 fixes into a single implementation phase.
Implementation plan with 4 phases (3.5h total) to fix pgrep self-match bug, add opencode detection, re-enable the sleep inhibitor service, and document toggle interface

active_projects.completion_summary: Created docs/neovim.md as authoritative Neovim documentation, dissolved redundant NOTES.md, added cross-references linking nix inline comments to docs/, and formalized documentation conventions in docs/README.md.

active_projects.description: Investigate and fix Gmail OAuth2 token expiry - tokens keep expiring requiring repeated re-authentication with himalaya account configure gmail

artifacts.summary: Comprehensive analysis of Gmail OAuth2 token expiry causes and solutions

Updated memory assessment shows system usage dropped from 80% to 50%. OpenCode is a new significant consumer (~1.7 GB). Infrastructure remains fully operational. One critical spike to 99% on 2026-03-29 recovered without intervention. No system changes recommended; focus on OpenCode session hygiene.
3-phase plan (2 hours, simple complexity) to extend claude-memory-tracker for OpenCode, add opencode to earlyoom prefer list, and validate infrastructure

memory_candidates.content: OpenCode has emerged as a new major memory consumer (~1.7 GB across 3 instances), comparable to Claude Code. The existing claude-memory-tracker only matches claude processes, making OpenCode invisible to process-specific memory tracking. Future memory monitoring should track both Claude and OpenCode, or use a broader pattern like AI assistant process matching.

On 2026-03-29 the system experienced a critical memory spike to 99% with swap at 86%, yet recovered fully without earlyoom intervention. This validates that the zram + swapfile + VM tuning infrastructure from Task 39 is resilient even under extreme pressure. The system can handle 99% memory utilization without hard failure.
